rulings.Case Title: GERARD MOONEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant fails to shift burden of production for defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff could not prove the work described by plaintiff did not expose him to asbestos. There is no D'Amico issue regarding plaintiff's declaration. Aye is qualified to express the opinion stated. Plaintiff's and Aye's declarations also create triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ZELONIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

rulings.Case Title: MALIGI TUITASI JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to sustain burden of proof. Defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff is unable to prove that the bags of insulation described by plaintiff contained asbestos. The Gregory declaration lacks foundation for the statement made. The motion for summary adjudication is denied for defendant's special interrogatories do not require plaintiff to delineate evidence that supports each of plaintiff's causes of action. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Pltfs' Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Discharging Interpleader And Awarding Fees To Alliance Title Co. And Request For Release Of Remainder Of Interpledf Unds If Reconsideration Is Denied In All Other Respects

rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION VS. NEWELL RUBBERMAID, INC. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFF MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Stanley Works. The settlement is approved. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA SCHAEFER et al VS. WILBUR WOO INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltfs Motion Seeking Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 18. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA SCHAEFER and JEFFREY CRANDALL'S Motion Seeking Leave To Amend. A hearing is required. Plaintiffs have not presented a declaration that identifies the facts plaintiff discovered in the course of discovery, when the facts were discovered and how it is those facts support plaintiffs proposed amendment. Plaintiff has merely attached documents to a declaration; that is not a sufficient showing as to the timeliness of the request or how it is the facts discovered give rise to a viable cause of action. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD E. GRAMMENS VS. NEWMARK PACIFIC, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS NEWMARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 16. CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD GRAMMENS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Cross-defendant has failed to shift the burden of production. Grammens has failed to demonstrate that Newmark Pacific is unable to prove each of its claims. There also are triable issues of fact as to whether the June 8 agreement was procured by fraud, as to Ms. Finigan's authority to sign the agreement, as to the knowledge of Newmark Pacific so as to make effective the release in the June 8 letter and the need for Grammens to disclose the details of the KMI transaction. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SASCO, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 19. DEFENDANT BOVIS LEND LEASE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts demonstrating that plaintiff's business has been disrupted. In plaintiff's opposition, plaintiff states the bidding process is not complete. As a consequence, it is not clear how it is that plaintiff has been injured. The demurrer is overruled as to the 2nd cause of action which states a claim for injunctive relief to preclude future disclosure of plaintiff's proprietary information. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: BROADWAY PROPERTIES LLC VS. WE PARK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 17, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANT WE PARK, INC'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Completion Of Arbitration. The motion is denied without prejudice because Powell Partners has not sought to intervene as an indispensable party. Should Powell Partners make that request and should the Court grant the motion, the request for a stay then could be considered. =(302/PJM)
